records.raw_text: As part of the Bronx Bus Network Redesign planning process, MTA and NYCDOT conducted an analysis of major Bronx corridors to identify streets where bus lanes and other bus priority treatments would provide the biggest benefit to Bronx bus riders.

Bx41 Select Bus Service launched June 30, 2013 on Webster Avenue in the Bronx. Phase 1 (2013 improvements) complete; Phase 2 (2015-16 capital project) in design.
SBS project on Webster Avenue from E 149 St/3 Av (The Hub) to E Gun Hill Rd, split into Phase 1 (2013 improvements) and Phase 2 (2015-16 Capital Project)

Bx41 Select Bus Service along Webster Avenue launched June 30, 2013, with off-board fare collection, offset bus lanes, intersection traffic improvements, and enhanced stations.

Addition of southbound bus lane resulted in 37% improvement in bus speeds in the PM Peak. Pedestrian safety enhancements calmed turns onto Cross Bronx Expwy. Pedestrian island to be installed in Spring 2020.
Improvements on Webster Avenue between E 176 St and E 174 St including bus lane gap fill, pedestrian island, painted curb extensions, right turn lane with qwik kurb, and signage
